title: Chat Message Drafts (Per-Conversation Composer State Persistence)
description: Unsent composer text (including reply context, mention state, and formatting entities) is automatically saved as a draft when a user navigates away from a conversation. Draft is restored when user returns. Drafts are per-conversation, per-device. Multi-device sync optional (trade-off: see below). Distinct from content-creation drafts (reels/posts) — this spec covers chat message composer state only.

scope_clarification.in_scope: Chat message composer text + reply_to_message_id + entities (bold/italic/mention/code) + cursor position
scope_clarification.out_of_scope: Reel drafts, story drafts, post drafts — handled by separate content-creation pipeline

schema.device_id: UUID FK → user_devices.id NOT NULL (if device-scoped) OR NULL (if cloud-synced)
schema.text: TEXT (max 4096 chars, matching message max length)

data_model.unique_constraint: UNIQUE (user_id, conversation_id, device_id) — one draft per user per conversation per device
data_model.rls: SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE restricted to owner: user_id = auth.uid()
data_model.index: idx_chat_drafts_user_conv ON chat_drafts(user_id, conversation_id) WHERE device_id IS NULL OR device_id = current_device_id

device_local.description: Draft stored in IndexedDB/SQLite on device. No server sync. Zero latency, zero bandwidth. Lost if device cleared.
device_local.trade_off: Draft not available on other devices.

cloud_synced.description: Draft synced to server, available across devices. Updated on each keystroke (debounced 2s).
cloud_synced.trade_off: Latency on first keystroke after debounce. Additional DB write amplification.
cloud_synced.conflict_resolution: Last-write-wins based on updated_at. No vector clocks needed — single-user, multi-device conflict is rare and low-stakes.

sync_strategy.recommended: Device-local by default with optional cloud sync (Settings → Privacy → Sync drafts)

save_draft.behavior: UPSERT on (user_id, conversation_id). Empty text → DELETE draft (cleared draft).
save_draft.idempotency: Last-write-wins. No idempotency key needed — overwrite is safe.
save_draft.rate_limit: 10 req/min per user (debounced on client, enforced on server)

get_draft.cache: SWR on client: serve stale immediately, revalidate in background

delete_draft.trigger: Message sent OR user clears composer manually
delete_draft.also_triggered_by: PUT with empty text body (idempotent empty = delete)

bulk_fetch.description: Returns all non-empty drafts for the authenticated user. Used on app startup to show draft indicators in chat list.

chat_list_integration.draft_indicator: In conversation list: if draft exists for a conversation, show 'Draft:' prefix in last-message preview (styled in red/accent color). updated_at used for list sorting — conversation with draft bubbles up if no newer messages.
chat_list_integration.no_draft_after_send: On successful message send, DELETE draft atomically before confirming send to client to prevent stale draft restoration.

security.draft_injection: Draft text validated on save (same rules as message text: max length, no null bytes). Entities validated for offset/length bounds against text length.
security.orphaned_drafts: Cron job purges drafts for deleted conversations hourly. Cascade delete on conversation/user deletion.

failure_handling.save_failure: Client retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff. On persistent failure: persist to localStorage as fallback. Show 'Draft not saved' indicator.
failure_handling.reply_target_deleted: On draft restore: if reply_to_message_id refers to deleted message, restore text only, clear reply context. Show 'Reply target was deleted' toast.

metrics: gauge: chat_draft.active_count (total non-empty drafts across all users)
